tagline: Work with Bitbucket Cloud repositories from the command line.

global_flags.name: credential
global_flags.summary: Use a saved Bitbucket connector credential.

global_flags.name: limit
global_flags.summary: Maximum records to emit for stream commands.

global_flags.name: max-bytes
global_flags.summary: Maximum bytes for direct-read JSON responses.

commands.path: repo list
repo view
repo create
commands.risk: Creates a repository in the configured workspace.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute. Typed confirmation may be required by policy.

commands.path: repo delete
commands.notes: Repository deletion is destructive admin behavior and is not exposed as a connector write.

commands.path: repo clone
commands.notes: Depends on local git and filesystem state; no local git executor is enabled.

commands.path: branch list
commit list
tag list
pull-request list
pull-request view
pull-request create
commands.risk: Creates a visible pull request in the configured repository.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

commands.path: pull-request merge
commands.risk: Merges code into the destination branch.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute. Typed confirmation may be required by policy.

commands.path: pull-request decline
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

commands.path: issue list
issue view
issue create
commands.risk: Creates a visible issue in the configured repository.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

commands.path: issue edit
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

commands.path: issue comment
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.
commands.notes: Bitbucket comment bodies require nested content objects; use reverse ETL writes once nested body mapping is modeled.

commands.path: pipeline list
pipeline view
pipeline run
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

commands.path: pipeline stop
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

commands.path: deployment list
download list
download get
commands.notes: Binary download to local filesystem requires explicit max-byte, destination, overwrite, and archive policies before execution.

commands.path: webhook list
webhook create
commands.risk: Creates an outbound webhook that can send repository events to an external URL.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute. URL policy review is required.

commands.path: webhook delete
commands.risk: Deletes an existing webhook and may interrupt downstream automation.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute. Typed confirmation may be required by policy.

commands.path: branch-restriction list
branch-restriction create
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute. Typed confirmation may be required for admin policy changes.

commands.path: branch-restriction delete
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute. Typed confirmation is required for admin policy changes.
commands.notes: Blocked until branch restriction delete confirmation UX is modeled.

commands.path: workspace list
project list
snippet list
snippet create
commands.risk: Creates a Bitbucket snippet and may publish code or text content.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute. Content redaction review is required.

commands.path: auth status
commands.notes: Use `pm credentials inspect <name> --redacted`; this metadata does not read secrets.

commands.path: config view
commands.notes: Connector-local CLI configuration is not implemented.

commands.path: api
commands.summary: Call an arbitrary Bitbucket API endpoint
commands.notes: Generic raw API calls are forbidden. Add reviewed direct-read or reverse-ETL operations instead.

commands.path: operation put addon
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute; typed confirmation is required for destructive/admin/sensitive operations.

commands.path: operation delete addon
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute; typed confirmation is required for destructive/admin/sensitive operations.

commands.path: operation get addon addon-key client-key
commands.summary: Get the client key of a Connect addon

commands.path: operation get hook-events
operation get hook-events subject-type
operation get repositories

flags.name: after
flags.summary:
Filter the results to include only repositories created on or
after this [ISO-8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601)
 timestamp. Example: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ`

flags.name: role
flags.summary:
Filters the result based on the authenticated user's role on each repository.

* **member**: returns repositories to which the user has explicit read access
* **contributor**: returns repositories to which the user has explicit write access
* **admin**: returns repositories to which the user has explicit administrator access
* **owner**: returns all repositories owned by the current user

flags.name: q
flags.summary:
Query string to narrow down the response as per [filtering and sorting](/cloud/bitbucket/rest/intro/#filtering).
`role` parameter must also be specified.

flags.name: sort
flags.summary: Field by which the results should be sorted as per [filtering and sorting](/cloud/bitbucket/rest/intro/#filtering).

flags.summary:

* **member**: returns repositories to which the user has explicit read access
* **contributor**: returns repositories to which the user has explicit write access
* **admin**: returns repositories to which the user has explicit administrator access
* **owner**: returns all repositories owned by the current user

commands.path: operation get repositories workspace repo-slug diff spec

flags.name: context
flags.summary: Generate diffs with <n> lines of context instead of the usual three.

flags.name: path
flags.summary:
Limit the diff to a particular file (this parameter
can be repeated for multiple paths).

flags.name: binary
flags.summary: Generate diffs that include binary files, true if omitted.

flags.name: renames
flags.summary: Whether to perform rename detection, true if omitted.

flags.name: merge
flags.summary:
This parameter is deprecated. The 'topic' parameter should be used
instead. The 'merge' and 'topic' parameters cannot be both used at
the same time.

If true, the source commit is merged into the
destination commit, and then a diff from the
destination to the merge result is returned. If false,
a simple 'two dot' diff between the source and
destination is returned. True if omitted.

flags.name: topic
flags.summary:
If true, returns 2-way 'three-dot' diff.
This is a diff between the source commit and the merge base
of the source commit and the destination commit.
If false, a simple 'two dot' diff between the source and
destination is returned.

If omitted, defaults to true, ie. a 2 way 'three-dot'
diff is returned.

commands.path: operation get repositories workspace repo-slug refs

flags.name: q
flags.summary:
Query string to narrow down the response as per
[filtering and sorting](/cloud/bitbucket/rest/intro/#filtering).

flags.name: sort
flags.summary:
Field by which the results should be sorted as per
[filtering and sorting](/cloud/bitbucket/rest/intro/#filtering). The `name`
field is handled specially for refs in that, if specified as the sort field, it
uses a natural sort order instead of the default lexicographical sort order. For example,
it will return ['1.1', '1.2', '1.10'] instead of ['1.1', '1.10', '1.2'].

commands.path: operation get repositories workspace repo-slug refs branches

flags.name: q

Query string to narrow down the response as per

flags.summary:
Field by which the results should be sorted as per
[filtering and sorting](/cloud/bitbucket/rest/intro/#filtering). The `name`
field is handled specially for branches in that, if specified as the sort field, it
uses a natural sort order instead of the default lexicographical sort order. For example,
it will return ['branch1', 'branch2', 'branch10'] instead of ['branch1', 'branch10', 'branch2'].

commands.summary: Get the root directory of the main branch

flags.name: format
flags.summary: Instead of returning the file's contents, return the (json) meta data for it.

commands.path: operation post repositories workspace repo-slug src
commands.summary: Create a commit by uploading a file
commands.risk: Create a commit by uploading a file
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute; typed confirmation is required for destructive/admin/sensitive operations.

commands.path: operation get repositories workspace repo-slug src commit path

flags.name: format
flags.summary: If 'meta' is provided, returns the (json) meta data for the contents of the file.  If 'rendered' is provided, returns the contents of a non-binary file in HTML-formatted rendered markup. The 'rendered' option only supports these filetypes: `.md`, `.markdown`, `.mkd`, `.mkdn`, `.mdown`, `.text`, `.rst`, and `.textile`. Since Git does not generally track what text encoding scheme is used, this endpoint attempts to detect the most appropriate character encoding. While usually correct, determining the character encoding can be ambiguous which in exceptional cases can lead to misinterpretation of the characters. As such, the raw element in the response object should not be treated as equivalent to the file's actual contents.

flags.name: q
flags.summary: Optional filter expression as per [filtering and sorting](/cloud/bitbucket/rest/intro/#filtering).

flags.name: sort
flags.summary: Optional sorting parameter as per [filtering and sorting](/cloud/bitbucket/rest/intro/#sorting-query-results).

flags.name: max-depth
flags.summary: If provided, returns the contents of the repository and its subdirectories recursively until the specified max_depth of nested directories. When omitted, this defaults to 1.

help_topics.name: safety

help_topics.name: coverage
